title: Capital-Lowercase speed mix
description: Real text with names and sentence starts — Shift drill at speed
text: Sarah and David met James at the New York office. Lisa from Boston joined later. They discussed Q3 plans, the European launch, and the partnership with Tokyo. Maria took notes. Friday wrap-up.

tips.text: Press Shift just before the letter, never after. The timing is the whole game with capitals.
Names, places, quarter numbers: real writing is full of them. This is the text you will actually type at work.

tips.target: Sarah and David met James at the New York office. Lisa from Boston joined later. They

tips.text: If Shift keeps breaking your stride, run a handful of capitals on their own until the hand-off goes quiet.

tips.target: discussed Q3 plans, the European launch, and the partnership with Tokyo. Maria took notes. Friday wrap-up.

character_tips.anna: Shift timing is what separates a fair typist from a fast one. Smooth the Shift and the speed smooths with it. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Half of business writing is names. Learn capitals at full pace and nothing slows you. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Leave Caps Lock alone. A clean Shift, pressed in time, does the job. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Pinky down, finger taps, then release. Gentle but sure. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
